hero.name: Ghazal Shabestani Monfared
hero.intro: Biomedical engineer bringing structure to complex projects. I bridge science and execution — coordinating teams, tracking deliverables, and making collaboration seamless.

paragraphs: I'm a PMP-certified biomedical engineer with hands-on experience coordinating projects in both startup and academic environments. Working at the intersection of biology and engineering, I naturally took on responsibilities beyond pure R&D — coordinating cross-functional teams, aligning priorities, tracking timelines, and improving workflows.
I thrive in roles where I can work closely with motivated, collaborative teams, support onboarding and communication, and build systems that make work clearer and more organized.
I'm particularly interested in opportunities in healthtech, startups, and mission-driven companies where I can combine my technical background with strong organizational and people-focused skills.

bullets: Took ownership of tracking timelines, tasks, and deliverables, helping the team meet fabrication deadlines consistently.
Contributed to the fabrication of 200+ microfluidic chips, supporting continuous prototyping of a cell sorting platform for cell therapy applications.
Conducted thorough testing and evaluation of prototypes and complete systems, ensuring quality standards.
Led project planning and execution, managing timelines and resources for successful outcomes.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to align project goals and enhance development processes.
Designed and fabricated a wound healing-on-a-chip system for biomedical applications.
Conducted rigorous testing, evaluation, and optimization of prototypes to enhance functionality.
Utilized cell culture techniques to support experimental validation and system development.
Developed strong project management and organizational skills navigating academic research processes.
Selected for the Technology Leveraging Workshop (Bootcamp for Scientists) focused on translating scientific ideas into real-world applications.
Awarded two Best Pitch prizes (€5,000 + Wild Card) for an innovative project recognized for clarity and impact.

education.school: Mazandaran University of Science and Technology

publications: Microfluidic and Lab on-a-Chip Systems for Cutaneous Wound Healing Studies
An on-chip wound healing assay fabricated by xurography for evaluation of dermal fibroblast cell migration and wound closure

contact.headline: Get in Touch
contact.text: I'm open to project coordination and management roles, especially in healthtech and innovation-driven environments. Let's connect.
